clinical_indication: Chronic low back pain with radicular symptoms; suspected lumbar radiculopathy

encounter_note.title: Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style)
encounter_note.text:
SUBJECTIVE: Jordan Rivera is a 47-year-old presenting with low back pain radiating down the left leg, ongoing for approximately 8 weeks. Pain is worse with prolonged sitting and bending. She reports difficulty sitting through a full workday and interrupted sleep due to pain. She denies recent trauma, fever, unexplained weight loss, saddle anesthesia, and bowel or bladder dysfunction. No history of cancer.

OBJECTIVE: Straight-leg raise is positive on the left at 40 degrees. Sensation decreased over the left lateral calf. Strength 5/5 throughout. Reflexes symmetric.

ASSESSMENT: Suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16).

PLAN: Naproxen 500 mg twice daily is listed on the medication list. Referral to physical therapy is in place. MRI lumbar spine without contrast ordered to evaluate for nerve root compression and to assess candidacy for epidural steroid injection or surgical referral if conservative measures fail. Return in 4 weeks or sooner if red-flag symptoms develop.

encounter_transcript.text: Clinician: How long has the pain been going on now? Patient: It's been about two months, and it shoots down my left leg. Clinician: Any numbness around the groin, trouble with bladder or bowels, fevers, weight loss? Patient: No, nothing like that. Clinician: Okay. Let's get an MRI of your lower back to see what's going on with that nerve.

policy.policy_title: Medical Necessity Policy: Lumbar Spine MRI

criteria.label: Appropriate diagnosis or indication
criteria.requirement:
The record documents a clinical indication appropriate for lumbar spine MRI,
such as suspected lumbar radiculopathy, with a corresponding diagnosis code.

criteria.label: Symptom duration of at least six weeks
criteria.requirement:
Symptoms (low back pain and/or radicular symptoms) have persisted for at
least six (6) weeks, with the duration explicitly documented.

criteria.label: Completed and failed conservative treatment
criteria.requirement:
The patient has **completed** at least six (6) weeks of conservative
treatment — such as physical therapy, anti-inflammatory medication, and/or a
structured home-exercise program — **without sufficient improvement**.
A referral to therapy or a prescription alone does not satisfy this
criterion; completion and failure must both be documented.

criteria.label: Relevant neurologic or examination findings
criteria.requirement:
The record documents neurologic or physical-examination findings consistent
with the indication (e.g., positive straight-leg raise, dermatomal sensory
change, motor weakness, or reflex asymmetry).

criteria.label: Red-flag screening
criteria.requirement:
The record documents screening for red-flag conditions (e.g., trauma, cancer
history, unexplained weight loss, fever, saddle anesthesia, bowel or bladder
dysfunction), with findings noted as present or absent.

criteria.label: Functional limitation
criteria.requirement:
The record documents a functional limitation attributable to the symptoms
(e.g., limitation of work, activities of daily living, or sleep).

criteria.label: Clinical rationale for MRI
criteria.requirement:
The record documents why MRI results are expected to change management
(e.g., evaluation for surgical or interventional candidacy).

clarification_questions.question: Have you completed at least six weeks of physical therapy, anti-inflammatory medication, or a home-exercise program without sufficient improvement?
clarification_questions.why_needed: The chart shows an NSAID on the medication list and a physical-therapy referral, but the policy requires documentation that at least six weeks of conservative treatment were completed without sufficient improvement. A referral or a prescription alone does not establish completion or failure.
clarification_questions.suggested_action: Confirm completion and outcome of conservative therapy; AuthLens will record your answer verbatim and re-evaluate.

clarifications.response: Patient completed six weeks of physical therapy and daily NSAID therapy without meaningful improvement.

sections.title: Patient and request summary
sections.body: Jordan Rivera (synthetic) (patient id pt-demo-001, born 1979-04-02, sex female) — request for MRI lumbar spine without contrast (CPT 72148) under policy MHP-IMG-2201 (Meridian Health Plans (fictional)). All statements below are drawn from cited chart evidence approved for disclosure.

sections.title: Requested service
sections.body: MRI lumbar spine without contrast — CPT 72148. Modality: MRI. Body site: lumbar spine.

sections.title: Clinical indication
sections.body: Chronic low back pain with radicular symptoms; suspected lumbar radiculopathy (clinician-stated indication; ICD-10 codes as provided by the clinician: M54.16, M54.41).

sections.title: Medical-necessity narrative
sections.body: An appropriate clinical indication with a diagnosis code is documented ("Onset approximately 8 weeks ago; suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16)"; "ASSESSMENT: Suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16)."). Duration is explicitly documented ("Onset approximately 8 weeks ago; suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16)"; "SUBJECTIVE: Jordan Rivera is a 47-year-old presenting with low back pain radiating down the left leg, ongoing for approximately 8 weeks."), meeting the 6-week requirement. Completion of conservative treatment without sufficient improvement is documented in the clinician's clarification ("Patient completed six weeks of physical therapy and daily NSAID therapy without meaningful improvement."). Examination findings are documented ("Straight-leg raise: positive on left at 40 degrees"; "OBJECTIVE: Straight-leg raise is positive on the left at 40 degrees."), consistent with the stated indication. Red-flag screening is explicitly documented with findings noted ("She denies recent trauma, fever, unexplained weight loss, saddle anesthesia, and bowel or bladder dysfunction."; "No history of cancer."). Functional impact is documented only as a patient-reported statement ("She reports difficulty sitting through a full workday and interrupted sleep due to pain."), without further characterization of frequency or severity. The record documents how the results are expected to change management ("MRI lumbar spine without contrast ordered to evaluate for nerve root compression and to assess candidacy for epidural steroid injection or surgical referral if conservative measures fail.").

LM-3 — Completed and failed conservative treatment [met]: "Patient completed six weeks of physical therapy and daily NSAID therapy without meaningful improvement." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style))
LM-4 — Relevant neurologic or examination findings [met]: "Straight-leg raise: positive on left at 40 degrees" (Straight-leg raise: positive on left at 40 degrees); "OBJECTIVE: Straight-leg raise is positive on the left at 40 degrees." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style)); "Sensation decreased over the left lateral calf." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style)); "Reflexes symmetric." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style))
LM-5 — Red-flag screening [met]: "She denies recent trauma, fever, unexplained weight loss, saddle anesthesia, and bowel or bladder dysfunction." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style)); "Clinician: Any numbness around the groin, trouble with bladder or bowels, fevers, weight loss?" (Encounter transcript); "No history of cancer." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style))
LM-6 — Functional limitation [weak]: "She reports difficulty sitting through a full workday and interrupted sleep due to pain." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style))
LM-7 — Clinical rationale for MRI [met]: "MRI lumbar spine without contrast ordered to evaluate for nerve root compression and to assess candidacy for epidural steroid injection or surgical referral if conservative measures fail." (Clinic note - low back pain follow-up (synthetic, Abridge-style))

sections.title: Human review
sections.body: WARNING — human review required. This packet is a machine-generated draft assembled from cited chart evidence. It has not been sent to any payer and cannot be sent by this system; documentation readiness is not a prediction of payer approval. A clinician must review every statement, resolve the remaining gaps, and complete the attestation. Requires clinician review before submission.

claims.text: An appropriate clinical indication with a diagnosis code is documented ("Onset approximately 8 weeks ago; suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16)"; "ASSESSMENT: Suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16).").

Duration is explicitly documented ("Onset approximately 8 weeks ago; suspected lumbar radiculopathy (M54.16)"; "SUBJECTIVE: Jordan Rivera is a 47-year-old presenting with low back pain radiating down the left leg, ongoing for approximately 8 weeks."), meeting the 6-week requirement.

Completion of conservative treatment without sufficient improvement is documented in the clinician's clarification ("Patient completed six weeks of physical therapy and daily NSAID therapy without meaningful improvement.").

Examination findings are documented ("Straight-leg raise: positive on left at 40 degrees"; "OBJECTIVE: Straight-leg raise is positive on the left at 40 degrees."), consistent with the stated indication.

Red-flag screening is explicitly documented with findings noted ("She denies recent trauma, fever, unexplained weight loss, saddle anesthesia, and bowel or bladder dysfunction."; "No history of cancer.").

Functional impact is documented only as a patient-reported statement ("She reports difficulty sitting through a full workday and interrupted sleep due to pain."), without further characterization of frequency or severity.

The record documents how the results are expected to change management ("MRI lumbar spine without contrast ordered to evaluate for nerve root compression and to assess candidacy for epidural steroid injection or surgical referral if conservative measures fail.").

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-1 ("Appropriate diagnosis or indication") requires: The record documents a clinical indication appropriate for lumbar spine MRI,
such as suspected lumbar radiculopathy, with a corresponding diagnosis code.

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-2 ("Symptom duration of at least six weeks") requires: Symptoms (low back pain and/or radicular symptoms) have persisted for at
least six (6) weeks, with the duration explicitly documented.

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-3 ("Completed and failed conservative treatment") requires: The patient has **completed** at least six (6) weeks of conservative
treatment — such as physical therapy, anti-inflammatory medication, and/or a
structured home-exercise program — **without sufficient improvement**.
A referral to therapy or a prescription alone does not satisfy this
criterion; completion and failure must both be documented.

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-4 ("Relevant neurologic or examination findings") requires: The record documents neurologic or physical-examination findings consistent
with the indication (e.g., positive straight-leg raise, dermatomal sensory
change, motor weakness, or reflex asymmetry).

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-5 ("Red-flag screening") requires: The record documents screening for red-flag conditions (e.g., trauma, cancer
history, unexplained weight loss, fever, saddle anesthesia, bowel or bladder
dysfunction), with findings noted as present or absent.

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-6 ("Functional limitation") requires: The record documents a functional limitation attributable to the symptoms
(e.g., limitation of work, activities of daily living, or sleep).

Policy MHP-IMG-2201 criterion LM-7 ("Clinical rationale for MRI") requires: The record documents why MRI results are expected to change management
(e.g., evaluation for surgical or interventional candidacy).
